trigger: Trump AI deregulation executive order and H200 sales to China

trigger: Trump AI deregulation executive order and H200 sales to China
quote: By the end of the week (when the Executive Order is expected), American citizens will have almost no route to protection from the consequences of AI
claim: After Trump's executive order preempting state AI laws, American citizens will have almost no legal protection from the consequences of AI.

trigger: Trump signs executive order blocking state AI regulation
quote: My own hunch is that all this is going to blow up on Trump (or at least on his heir apparent J.D. Vance) badly.
claim: Trump's AI deregulation policy will backfire politically on Trump or J.D. Vance.

trigger: Trump signs executive order blocking state AI regulation
quote: Anything that goes wrong, from AI-fueled cybercrime to bioweapons attacks facilitated by AI to teen suicides apparently linked to GenAI will be on his hands, and his reputation.
claim: Trump will bear political responsibility for any harms from AI including cybercrime, bioweapons facilitated by AI, and teen suicides linked to GenAI, due to his deregulation.

trigger: Trump signs executive order blocking state AI regulation
quote: he will also be closely tied to any AI-tinged economic debacle that happens on his watch.
claim: Trump will be politically tied to any AI-related economic downturn that occurs during his administration.

trigger: Trump signs executive order blocking state AI regulation
quote: The opposite of overregulation is underregulation; here we now essentially have zero regulation. What we obviously need is something in between, fostering innovation but also protecting our citizens.
claim: The US needs AI regulation that balances innovation with citizen protection, rather than the current state of essentially zero regulation.

trigger: Trump executive order on AI and China competition claims

claim: The generative AI race between the US and China is not and will never be winner-take-all; it will be more like Coke vs Pepsi.

trigger: Trump executive order on AI and China competition claims
quote: I literally don't see any plausible scenario in which either nation outright 'wins' the generative AI race to the exclusion of the other.
claim: There is no plausible scenario in which either the US or China outright wins the generative AI race to the exclusion of the other.

trigger: Trump executive order on AI and China competition claims
quote: since everybody is following the same playbook—ever more massive data, ever more massive servers, fed into ever-larger large language models—nobody has a real technical advantage here.
claim: No country has a real technical advantage in generative AI because everyone is following the same playbook of scaling LLMs with more data and compute.

trigger: Trump executive order on AI and China competition claims
quote: Right now, as it happens, China is at most a few months behind; I expect the lead to be slight and to flip back and forth, with nobody retaining a sizeable a lead for long.
claim: China is at most a few months behind the US in generative AI, and the lead will flip back and forth with neither side retaining a sizable lead.

trigger: Trump executive order on AI and China competition claims
quote: genAI models are, too. Building our AI policy around a fantasy that we are somehow going to crush China in LLM war (or vice versa!) is misguided.
claim: Generative AI models are commodities, and building US AI policy around the fantasy of crushing China in an LLM war is misguided.

trigger: Trump executive order on AI and China competition claims
quote: it may be that the real winner is whichever country doesn't overextend itself to the point of financial ruin, in a foolish effort to win a race that can't be won.
claim: In the AI race, the real winner may be whichever country avoids overextending itself financially rather than the one that spends the most.

trigger: Trump executive order on AI and China competition claims
quote: All the more so if LLMs turn out to be a dud, or if LLMs are replaced by smaller, more efficient systems that don't demand such immense amounts of infrastructure.
claim: The risk of overinvestment in AI infrastructure is even greater if LLMs turn out to be a dud or are replaced by smaller, more efficient systems.

trigger: Owain Evans research on LLM vulnerabilities
quote: The problem with generative AI has always been that large language models associate patterns together without really understanding those patterns; it's statistics without comprehension.
claim: LLMs associate patterns without understanding them; they perform statistics without comprehension.

trigger: Owain Evans research on LLM vulnerabilities
quote: A lot of hallucinations are borne of exactly this kind of overgeneralization
claim: Many LLM hallucinations are caused by overgeneralization from spurious statistical correlations between words rather than genuine conceptual understanding.

trigger: Owain Evans research on LLM vulnerabilities
quote: it's not even that LLMs are picking up on real correlations in the world ... it's that the LLMs learn weird nth order correlations between words (rather than concepts)
claim: LLMs learn nth-order correlations between words rather than between real-world concepts, leading to bizarre and incorrect associations.

trigger: Owain Evans research on LLM vulnerabilities
quote: There is no way in Darwin's green earth that we are ever going to be able to patch what is likely to be an endless list of vulnerabilities.
claim: The security vulnerabilities of LLMs stemming from statistical correlation are effectively unpatchable due to being an endless list.

trigger: Owain Evans research on LLM vulnerabilities
quote: Putting society in the hands of giant, superficial correlation machines is not going to end well.
claim: Relying on LLMs (superficial correlation machines) for important societal functions will end badly.

trigger: NeurIPS 2025 conference
quote: One of the costliest mistakes in scientific history is coming to an end
claim: The belief that scaling LLMs would lead to AGI is one of the costliest mistakes in scientific history, and it is now coming to an end.

trigger: NeurIPS 2025 conference
quote: these systems would never be reliable enough, and that even with more data they would have trouble with hallucinations, factuality, reasoning, outliers, and generalization.
claim: LLMs will never be reliable enough, even with more data, and will continue to have problems with hallucinations, factuality, reasoning, outliers, and generalization.

trigger: NeurIPS 2025 conference
quote: The return on investment that people dream of is just not there, for most customers.
claim: The return on investment from generative AI is not materializing for most business customers.

trigger: NeurIPS 2025 conference
quote: If we are to build AGI, we are going to need to learn something from humans, how they reason and understand the physical world, and how they represent and acquire language and complex concepts.
claim: Building AGI requires learning from human cognition -- how humans reason, understand the physical world, and represent and acquire language and complex concepts.

trigger: NeurIPS 2025 conference
quote: Better late than never. Let's bring in the cognitive scientists, and stop fantasizing that data and compute will solve all our problems. The time for neurosymbolic AI and world models and causality is now.
claim: The AI field needs to bring in cognitive scientists and shift to neurosymbolic AI, world models, and causality research rather than continuing to rely on data and compute scaling.

trigger: Wall Street Journal and Bloomberg reports on AI debt

trigger: Wall Street Journal and Bloomberg reports on AI debt

trigger: Matt Shumer viral blog post on AI coding

claim: Claims that AI coding systems can write whole complex apps without errors are not supported by evidence and ignore reliability problems

trigger: Matt Shumer viral blog post on AI coding
quote: No AI system can reliably do every five-hour long task humans can do without error, or even close

trigger: Matt Shumer viral blog post on AI coding
quote: the criterion on that benchmark is 50% correct, not 100%, and that the benchmark is only about coding and not tasks in general
claim: The METR task-time benchmark that AI boosters cite uses a 50% success threshold and covers only coding, not general tasks

trigger: Matt Shumer viral blog post on AI coding
quote: coders sometimes imagine big productivity gains where they actually lost productivity
claim: Programmers using AI coding tools sometimes perceive large productivity gains when they have actually lost productivity

trigger: Matt Shumer viral blog post on AI coding
quote: Generally, the closer these systems are to appearing right, the more dangerous they become because people become increasingly at ease just trusting them [when they shouldn't]

trigger: Matt Shumer viral blog post on AI coding
quote: LLMs are certainly coding more, but it's not clear that the code they are creating is secure or trustworthy
claim: LLM-generated code is of uncertain security and trustworthiness despite increasing adoption

trigger: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman making big promises to FT

claim: As of December 2025, AI could only perform about 2.5% of human online tasks according to the Remote Labor Index

trigger: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman making big promises to FT
quote: Promises are cheap. And all the big tech CEOs have come to realize that making them costs them nothing.
claim: Big tech CEOs systematically make unfounded promises about AI capabilities because there are no consequences for doing so

trigger: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman making big promises to FT

claim: AI company valuations are driven by hype rather than fundamentals, following the Tesla model of promise-driven valuation

trigger: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman making big promises to FT

claim: Media outlets should provide more context about AI executives' conflicts of interest and track records when platforming their predictions

trigger: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman making big promises to FT

claim: Uncritical media amplification of AI hype could be an important contributing factor if the AI bubble collapses

trigger: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman making big promises to FT

claim: GPT-5's actual improvements over GPT-4 were far more modest than Microsoft CTO Kevin Scott's predictions

trigger: SoftBank CFO hedging on OpenAI investment; Nvidia pulling back $100B pledge

claim: OpenAI will eventually be seen as the WeWork of AI, and that moment is approaching.

trigger: SoftBank CFO hedging on OpenAI investment; Nvidia pulling back $100B pledge
quote: Google and Anthropic have each largely caught up; various Chinese companies are closing in.
claim: Google and Anthropic have largely caught up to OpenAI technically, and Chinese AI companies are closing in.

trigger: SoftBank CFO hedging on OpenAI investment; Nvidia pulling back $100B pledge
quote: more and more people are agreeing that AGI won't arrive this decade
claim: AGI will not arrive this decade.

trigger: SoftBank CFO hedging on OpenAI investment; Nvidia pulling back $100B pledge
quote: OpenAI is losing money every quarter; even by their own (optimistic) calculations, they won't make a profit for years. I doubt their runway would last much more than a year.
claim: OpenAI is losing money every quarter and its financial runway will likely not last much more than a year without new investment.

trigger: SoftBank CFO hedging on OpenAI investment; Nvidia pulling back $100B pledge
quote: If the venture capital dries up, they are toast. Talent will make a beeline for competitors.

trigger: SoftBank CFO hedging on OpenAI investment; Nvidia pulling back $100B pledge

trigger: SoftBank CFO hedging on OpenAI investment; Nvidia pulling back $100B pledge
quote: it's looking more and more like they will need one. Hopefully the US government will have the good sense not to bite; Google and Anthropic are doing just fine, without government handouts.
claim: OpenAI will likely need a government bailout, but the US government should not provide one since Google and Anthropic are doing fine without government support.

trigger: SoftBank CFO hedging on OpenAI investment; Nvidia pulling back $100B pledge
quote: OpenAI's lead is slipping, and now it looks their financing is, too.
claim: OpenAI's technical lead and financing are both deteriorating.

trigger: OpenAI complaining about IP theft; xAI gutting safety team

claim: OpenAI is one of the biggest IP thieves in history due to snarfing intellectual property of artists and writers.

trigger: xAI gutting safety operations

claim: Elon Musk's xAI has gutted its safety operations, which is dangerous given his reckless approach to winning the AI race.

trigger: Deepfake video advances; OpenClaw voice impersonation capabilities
quote: Anybody's appearance can (given sufficient data) be faked now, at any time, for almost nothing.
claim: Current deepfake technology can fake anyone's appearance at nearly zero cost given sufficient data.

trigger: Deepfake video advances; OpenClaw voice impersonation capabilities
quote: Because the tools for counterfeiting have gotten so good 2026 will almost certainly see more deepfaked scams like this than the rest of history combined.
claim: 2026 will see more deepfake scams than all prior years in history combined.

trigger: Deepfake video advances; OpenClaw voice impersonation capabilities
quote: Tell your representatives today, not tomorrow, that we must pass federal laws forbidding machine output from being presented as humans, and that we must develop the means to enforce those laws.
claim: The US must pass federal laws forbidding machine output from being presented as human and develop enforcement mechanisms.

trigger: Deepfake video advances; OpenClaw voice impersonation capabilities
quote: No use of the first person by chatbots, and no more deepfakes of living people's voices and images without their express consent, aside from carveouts for obvious parody and so on.

trigger: Deepfake video advances; OpenClaw voice impersonation capabilities
quote: we must not let corporate lobbyists thwart efforts to address all this.
claim: Corporate lobbyists are a threat to passing necessary AI deepfake regulation.

trigger: Deepfake video advances; OpenClaw voice impersonation capabilities
quote: Generative AI systems may still struggle to reason, as discussed here recently, but they were built for mimicry, and their mimicry has gotten to the point where we must do something now.
claim: Generative AI systems still struggle to reason but excel at mimicry, and that mimicry capability has reached a level requiring immediate regulatory action.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: Rumors that humanity has already achieved artificial general intelligence (AGI) have been greatly exaggerated.
claim: Claims that AGI has already been achieved are greatly exaggerated.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: Such claims confuse high performance on benchmarks (often gameable) with real world flexibility, which historically has been at the center of what it means to be an artificial general intelligence.
claim: Claims of AGI achievement confuse high benchmark performance with real-world flexibility, which is the historical core of the AGI concept.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: recent claims of achieving AGI rest on a conceptual error: conflating increasingly sophisticated statistical approximations with intelligence itself
claim: Recent claims of achieving AGI rest on a conceptual error that conflates statistical approximation with genuine intelligence.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: recent claims (Chen et al., 2026) about putative success on AGI hinge on redefining what AGI has historically meant
claim: Recent claims of AGI success depend on redefining AGI away from its original meaning of robust, flexible competence across environments.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: In recent years, however, some have tried to redefine AGI, weakening the term. We see no evidence that the original standards have been met.
claim: The original standards for AGI -- robustness, flexibility, adaptability, and transfer under novelty -- have not been met by any current AI system.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: benchmark success is a limited indicator of general intelligence. By design, benchmarks isolate narrow competencies and abstract away real-world context, making it difficult to distinguish genuine generalization from pattern recognition.
claim: Benchmark success is a limited indicator of general intelligence because benchmarks isolate narrow competencies and cannot distinguish genuine generalization from pattern recognition.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: systems have been trained more often directly on benchmarks ("teaching to the test") or on synthetic data closely resembling them. As a result, model development and data curation are optimized for benchmark success, producing systems that perform well under test conditions but degrade in real-world settings
claim: AI systems are increasingly trained directly on benchmarks or similar synthetic data, producing systems that perform well on tests but degrade in real-world settings.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: even state-of-the-art systems can reliably perform only a small fraction of the tasks required in typical human occupations
claim: Even state-of-the-art AI systems can reliably perform only a small fraction of the tasks required in typical human occupations.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: only a minority of firms report economically meaningful returns from AI deployment
claim: Only a minority of firms report economically meaningful returns from AI deployment.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: the core properties of general intelligence – flexibility, generality, and reliability – remain elusive
claim: The core properties of general intelligence -- flexibility, generality, and reliability -- remain elusive in current AI systems.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: similar behaviors can be produced by fundamentally different processes, and that producing the right output does not imply the same cognitive capacities
claim: AI systems producing human-like outputs does not imply they possess the same cognitive capacities, because similar behaviors can arise from fundamentally different processes.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: models exhibited high confidence even when evidence was sparse, conflicting, or explicitly flagged as uncertain, whereas human judgments were strongly modulated by uncertainty, justification, and perceived costs of error
claim: LLMs exhibit high confidence even when evidence is sparse or conflicting, unlike humans who modulate judgments based on uncertainty and stakes.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: agreement at the level of outputs concealed substantial divergence in the underlying judgment processes, reflecting a systematic substitution of epistemic evaluation with generative plausibility
claim: Surface-level agreement between LLM and human outputs conceals substantial divergence in underlying judgment processes; LLMs substitute epistemic evaluation with generative plausibility.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: today's large language models lack persistent goals, struggle with long-horizon reasoning, and depend extensively on human scaffolding for task formulation, evaluation, and correction
claim: Current LLMs lack persistent goals, struggle with long-horizon reasoning, and depend extensively on human scaffolding.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: these results primarily reflect the ability to rapidly search, recombine, and iterate over existing techniques, rather than the emergence of genuinely novel or domain-general problem-solving strategies
claim: AI successes on open math problems reflect the ability to search and recombine existing techniques, not genuinely novel or domain-general problem-solving.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: current systems remain brittle, sensitive to prompt framing, and inconsistent outside curated evaluation settings
claim: Current AI systems remain brittle, sensitive to prompt framing, and inconsistent outside curated evaluation settings.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: Current AI systems are powerful and increasingly useful tools, but they do not exhibit the flexible, self-directed competence that the original concept of artificial general intelligence was intended to capture.
claim: Current AI systems are powerful and useful tools but do not exhibit the flexible, self-directed competence that AGI was intended to capture.

trigger: Nature article claiming AGI has arrived
quote: overestimating their cognitive capacities risks misallocating trust, responsibility, and authority. Confusing increasingly sophisticated statistical approximation with general intelligence is therefore not only a conceptual error, but a strategic misjudgment.
claim: Overestimating AI cognitive capacities risks misallocating trust, responsibility, and authority, making the conflation of statistical approximation with general intelligence a strategic misjudgment.

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution
quote: Generative AI has been inherently unreliable from the start; none of the problems that I warned about over the last half decade has been properly solved.
claim: Generative AI has been inherently unreliable from the start and none of its core problems have been properly solved.

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution
quote: Large language models still hallucinate, and they still make boneheaded errors; they still lack a proper concept of reality.
claim: LLMs still hallucinate, make boneheaded errors, and lack a proper concept of reality.

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution
quote: A recent survey called The Remote Labor Index found that they could only do 2.5% of human tasks, and that is a massive overestimate, since literally everything that requires physical labor was excluded.
claim: AI systems can only perform about 2.5% of human tasks (and that is an overestimate since physical labor was excluded).

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution
quote: Generative AI has its uses, but it hardly surprising that most business have struggled to find return on their investment, given the massive reliability issues that continue to plaguage it.
claim: Most businesses have struggled to find return on their generative AI investment due to massive reliability issues.

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution
quote: the economic impact may well be a lot smaller than many people were led to believe
claim: The economic impact of generative AI is likely much smaller than widely believed.

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution

claim: Generative AI has been marketed as tantamount to AGI when it is not.

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution
quote: there is just no way for the product they actually know how build — as opposed to the one they fantasize about — to live up to those expectations
claim: The AI products companies can actually build cannot live up to the AGI-level expectations being set for them.

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution
quote: my best guess is that generative AI will have done significantly more harm to society than good
claim: Generative AI will ultimately do significantly more harm to society than good.

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution

claim: Generative AI is ripping apart the educational system and information ecosystem, and flooding the zone with nonconsensual deepfake porn.

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution
quote: It is threatening the environment with data centers built on too much speculation.
claim: AI data centers built on speculation are threatening the environment.

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution
quote: It is leading some people into serious mental health issues.
claim: Generative AI is leading some people into serious mental health issues.

trigger: Washington Post report on overstated generative AI economic contribution
quote: And it may well lay waste to our economy, once banks and investors who bought the hype start to fall.
claim: Generative AI may lay waste to the economy once banks and investors who bought the hype start to fall.

trigger: Trump administration pressuring Anthropic for unrestricted military AI access
quote: it should be obvious that applying hallucinatory and unreliable Generative AI to weapon systems without humans in the loop could be catastrophic
claim: Applying unreliable generative AI to weapon systems without humans in the loop could be catastrophic.

trigger: Trump administration pressuring Anthropic for unrestricted military AI access
quote: Generative AI is NOT remotely reliable enough to make life or death decisions of magnitude.
claim: Generative AI is not remotely reliable enough to make life-or-death decisions.

trigger: Trump administration pressuring Anthropic for unrestricted military AI access

trigger: Trump administration pressuring Anthropic for unrestricted military AI access

claim: Embedding LLMs into critical systems must be done in a way that acknowledges and factors in their unreliability.

trigger: Trump administration pressuring Anthropic for unrestricted military AI access
quote: Spreading it everywhere without proper consideration could well lead to disaster.

trigger: Keith Payne nuclear crisis simulation study
quote: the nuclear taboo is no impediment to nuclear escalation by our models
claim: In simulated nuclear crises, AI models show no impediment from the nuclear taboo and readily escalate to nuclear options.

trigger: Hegseth demanding unrestricted Anthropic access by Friday 5pm

claim: Secretary Hegseth lacks a nuanced understanding of AI's strengths and limits and will not show restraint in its military application.

trigger: Hegseth demanding unrestricted Anthropic access by Friday 5pm
quote: AI policy, especially of this magnitude, is something that American people should have a say in. Congress should deliberate.
claim: AI policy of this magnitude should involve Congressional deliberation and public input, not unilateral Cabinet decisions.

trigger: Hegseth demanding unrestricted Anthropic access by Friday 5pm

trigger: Hegseth-Anthropic military AI standoff
quote: No LLM, anywhere, in its current form, should be considered for use in a fully lethal autonomous weapon system. It's ludicrous even to suggest it.

trigger: Trump denouncing Anthropic after nuclear weapons standoff

trigger: Trump denouncing Anthropic after nuclear weapons standoff

trigger: Trump denouncing Anthropic after nuclear weapons standoff

trigger: OpenAI new funding round

claim: OpenAI's latest investments are vendor financing dressed up as venture capital, not arms-length investments.

trigger: OpenAI new funding round

claim: OpenAI received 65% of all venture capital in one round despite never having made a profit and having no clear technical advantage.

trigger: OpenAI new funding round
quote: the product is unreliable (placing an upper bound on how much many people be willing to pay), it is expensive to operate, and they have no technical moat, which has led to price wars
claim: OpenAI struggles to profit for three reasons: the product is unreliable (capping willingness to pay), it is expensive to operate, and they have no technical moat (leading to price wars).

trigger: OpenAI new funding round

claim: Google and Anthropic have caught up to OpenAI, with Chinese companies not far behind and ahead on price.

trigger: OpenAI new funding round

claim: The conventional wisdom that OpenAI was unassailable has been proven wrong; OpenAI itself recognizes it is in a Code Red with Google close behind.

trigger: OpenAI new funding round
